danger_signals.visual_form: Penguin's thugs, clown henchmen, Catwoman animations
Catwoman emerges during stage progression; distinct sprite and behavior pattern
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated threat requiring adapted combat strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming enemy vehicles during Batmobile/Batskiboat sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Projectile/collision danger requiring evasion and counter-fire

danger_signals.visual_form: Stage select menu structure; 'Stage Clear' / 'Score Clear' title cards marking completion
danger_signals.meaning: Zone completion confirms survival of prior threat cluster

safety_signals.visual_form: 'Scene Clear' title card displayed after defeating enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression checkpoint; threat in zone neutralized

safety_signals.visual_form: Grappling gun and other collectable items in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Tools for expanding combat capability and traversal options

safety_signals.visual_form: Animated sequences (e.g., Catwoman escaping via 'kitty-litter' save)
safety_signals.meaning: Narrative progress; temporary narrative safety between active threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bat insignia on chest; Batarangs as signature weapon
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Batcave presence implied through equipment access; Batmobile/Batskiboat vehicles

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied henchmen sprites (thugs in dark clothing)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Penguin boss character with distinctive umbrella weapon
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple thug types with different attack patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss arena design; stage-specific thug deployment patterns

faction_indicators.color_identity: Purple/dark violet suit with distinct silhouette
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mid-stage boss form; separate animation set for defeat sequence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Building rooftop environment during first encounter

faction_indicators.color_identity: Bright, chaotic color palette (implied from 'Tall Clown' enemy type)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Test Tube enemies suggest chemical/comedic weaponry
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse enemy sprite set (B. Test Tube, etc.) indicating varied clown gang composition
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage progression through themed zones corresponding to villain territories

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Continuous enemy spawn; henchmen patrol/attack patterns visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Batman must engage in beat-em-up combat; thrown Batarangs in 'run and gun' sections

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arena transitions; boss sprite appears with distinct animation; stage music intensifies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat difficulty escalates; specialized attack patterns required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 'Scene Clear' title card overlay; transition cutscene (e.g., Catwoman escape sequence)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawning ceases; player control returns to narrative sequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Camera perspective shifts to vehicle-centric view; enemy vehicles rendered in lane-based formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Batmobile/Batskiboat control mode; player fires projectiles at approaching threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: End credits roll; western versions display 'You are the True Dark Knight' message on Mania difficulty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game freeze or completion screen; difficulty-based achievement text

environmental_storytelling.example: Penguin and Catwoman encounter zones use distinct visual themes and enemy spawn patterns tied to their criminal operations
Grappling gun collectibles placed in stages; their presence signals traversal challenges ahead and Batman's adaptive response to environment
Catwoman's defeat followed by 'saved by kitty-litter' sequence; visual storytelling conveys her character arc and survival motivation within film adaptation
Shift from beat-em-up to Batmobile/Batskiboat combat indicates zone control expanding beyond foot-combat territories into vehicular threat domains
'Scene Clear' / 'Score Clear' cards serve dual narrative-mechanical function: confirm stage victory and visually demarcate Gotham sub-regions as Batman conquers them

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Animated combat attacks; whip-based melee offense
Gets up weakened; deliberately jumps off building (west) / falls after defeat (JP version); 'kitty-litter' rescue implied
Patrol and attack in varied patterns; multiple sprite types indicate different threat profiles
Chemical/comedic weapon attacks (Test Tube enemy type); suggest Joker-faction aggression
Beat-em-up attacks; Batarang throw in run-and-gun sections; vehicle operation in driving stages
Idle during cutscenes; recovery animation between stages

notes: Batman Returns (SNES) is a multi-mechanic beat-em-up that communicates world-state primarily through enemy type variation, stage transitions, and distinct gameplay modes. Danger signals center on enemy presence and boss encounters, while safety is signaled through completion title cards and cutscene progression. Faction identity relies on villain-specific sprite design (Penguin, Catwoman, Clown henchmen) and arena theming rather than explicit color coding or sigils. Regional differences (JP vs. Western) affect cutscene sequencing for Catwoman's defeat but maintain consistent mechanical communication. The game uses vehicle sections (Batmobile/Batskiboat) to signal expanded threat scope beyond foot combat. Enemy naming conventions (e.g., 'Tall Clown' vs. 'Tall Crown' regional variant, 'B. Test Tube' vs. 'Test Tube') suggest faction-specific thug archetypes but lack detailed source documentation.
